for that discipline type. However, a one-time order; e.g., to give gamma globulin 
following exposure to hepatitis, would not be considered a need for "intermittent" skilled 
nursing care since a recurrence of the problem that would require this service is not 
medically predictable. 

Although most patients require services no more frequently than several times a week, 
Medicare will pay for part-time (as defined in §50.7) medically reasonable and necessary 
skilled nursing care 7
